ops.body:
A `ccx vcs ship` failed with a raw non-fast-forward rejection when a concurrent session advanced origin/main mid-ship, despite docs claiming in-call fetch+rebase handling. Root cause was three stacked gaps:

1. **jj lane TOCTOU**: `shipPushJJ` fetched, ancestor-checked, rebased, moved the bookmark, then pushed ‚Äî several subprocesses between fetch and push with no rejection classification and no retry. A push is a compare-and-swap; the window cannot be closed client-side, only absorbed by re-entering fetch‚Üírebase‚Üípush on a classified rejection.
2. **git lane had no protection at all**: `shipPushGit` was a bare `git push` ‚Äî no fetch, no rebase. The documented fetch-first behavior was jj-only.
3. **Conflicted-bookmark trap**: a failed jj push left the local bookmark already advanced (move precedes push), so even the documented manual re-run bounced ‚Äî the rerun's fetch saw local-moved + remote-moved, three-way-merged the ref into a conflicted bookmark, and ship's own heads>1 check refused.

Fix (landed 2026-07-23): typed `pushRejectedError` contract ‚Äî the invariant is *re-entrancy*, not entry-restoration ‚Äî with a 3-attempt shared retry loop keeping the last non-zero rebase count. jj rolls back only its own operation via targeted `jj op revert <op>` (NOT `jj op undo`, which does not exist on jj 0.43; NOT `jj op restore`, which is repo-wide and erases a concurrent session's interleaved operations ‚Äî both empirically verified). git gains fetch-first + `rebase --autostash` parity against the branch's resolved remote (`branch.<b>.remote`, fallback origin), with a `REBASE_HEAD` probe separating never-started rebases from conflicts and cleanup on `context.WithoutCancel`. `--amend` never retries: the git amend lane pushes plain first and force-pushes only with a lease pinned to the pre-amend HEAD ‚Äî an unqualified `--force-with-lease` is unsafe the moment anything else fetches (IDE background fetches refresh the tracking ref and turn the lease into a rubber stamp; reproduced on git 2.55). Classifiers match git's per-ref reason tokens `(non-fast-forward)`/`(fetch first)`/`(stale info)` with `[remote rejected]` as a veto, and jj's `unexpectedly moved on the remote` ‚Äî never `hint:` lines, never jj's ambiguous terminal `Failed to push some bookmarks` (also printed on permission failures), and never git tokens on the jj lane (jj drives git with `--porcelain` and prints its own report).

Meta-lesson: the wrong-verb bug (`jj op undo`) passed the entire unit suite because the fake jj's `case` had no default arm ‚Äî any unmatched argv silently exited 0. Fakes now exit 2 on unmatched argv; external-CLI verbs and stderr wordings need empirical grounding against the installed binary, not memory.
